Fixed in 8e31e95: MAX_ARRAY_INDEX_PAYLOAD + MAX_ARRAY_INDEX_ELEMENTS limits
Fixed in 8e31e95: two-generation backup rotation
Fixed in 8e31e95: isStateStructureValid() added
Fixed in 8e31e95: stderr warnings on corruption
All 3 MAJOR sub-beads fixed in 8e31e95
ci() uses execFileSync with argument array (no shell), plus null byte guard added
dist/ pre-flight check already present at install() lines 62-65
Error-code-specific handling already present: ENOENT, EACCES/EPERM, ENOSPC, generic fallback at lines 74-84

Fixed checksum double-serialize: save() now spreads the state object directly instead of re-parsing JSON, eliminating the fragile parse-serialize-parse-serialize round-trip. Checksum is computed over canonical JSON of state, then state+checksum is serialized once for the file.
Already fixed in prior commit (D02-fix comment on line 78). backupCurrentState() is already inside the withLock() body, making backup+write atomic under the lock.
Fixed lock file handle leak: wrapped handle.writeFile+close in try/finally in both acquireLock paths (loop body and force-remove retry). Handle is now always closed even if writeFile throws.

Fixed Windows PID check: isLockStale() now uses 30s timestamp threshold as PRIMARY check (stale if age >= 30s). PID liveness check (process.kill(pid,0)) is only used as SECONDARY on non-Windows (process.platform \!== 'win32'). On Windows, only timestamp is trusted.

Fixed infinite crash loop: stateStore.save(failed) in inject-context.ts error boundary is now wrapped in nested try/catch. If save throws, the error is silently caught and the failure prompt message is still returned to the user.
Added isAbsoluteOrUnsafePath() guard to reject Windows absolute paths (backslash, drive-letter) in file_exists gate predicate
Narrowed envOpt type from confusing union to { env?: Record<string,string> } and removed dead envPart guard
Fixed parseEnv regex to fallback-match when env: is last section (no flow:/done when: following)
Already implemented: findSimilarPredicate() imported and used in evaluate-completion.ts with fuzzy matching, tests verified
Replaced separate and/or search with findRightmostOperator() for true left-to-right associativity — a and b or c is now (a and b) or c
Added goal sanitization (collapse newlines to spaces) and spawn name validation (alphanumeric+hyphens only, throws on invalid)

Enhanced PreCompact hook: now uses renderFlowCompact()+renderFlowSummary() for token-efficient compaction context, adds awaiting_capture detection with capture retry prompt re-injection, includes debug logging. Test added for awaiting_capture scenario. All 56 hook tests pass.

Added compaction-aware re-injection to SessionStart hook. When state has awaiting_capture nodeProgress, re-emits buildCaptureRetryPrompt() in additionalContext so variable capture survives session resume after compaction. findAwaitingCapture()+findNodeById() helpers added. Integration test verifies capture prompt with nonce is included in stdout JSON.

Added debug/verbose mode. Created src/presentation/hooks/debug.ts with debug() helper checking PROMPT_LANGUAGE_DEBUG env var. Writes to stderr with [PL:debug] prefix. Added debug logging to session-start.ts (state load, cleanup, capture re-injection), user-prompt-submit.ts (state transitions, prompt modification, completion), and pre-compact.ts (flow preservation, capture detection). 4 unit tests for debug helper (env on/off/0/false). Set PROMPT_LANGUAGE_DEBUG=1 to enable.
renderNodeToDsl now escapes newlines to spaces in prompt/run text
Changed timeoutSeconds != null to !== undefined (0 no longer falsy)
Removed node:crypto from domain — nonce uses pure JS Math.random, createSessionState accepts optional nonce param
AutoAdvanceResult refactored to tagged union with kind: prompt|advance|pause
Foreach re-entry uses cached maxIterations from NodeProgress
Nonce increased from 32-bit to 128-bit (4 segments of 8 hex chars)

MCP research findings: (1) No existing MCP config (.mcp.json or tool_server) in the project — plugin uses hooks-only architecture via hooks.json. (2) An MCP tool like flow_capture_response(varName, value) would be more deterministic than XML tag capture since it bypasses text parsing entirely. (3) Benefits: eliminates tag spoofing risk (no nonce needed), removes capture retry loop, simplifies PostToolUse tag scanning. (4) Complexity: requires MCP server implementation (stdio transport), Claude Code plugin MCP support (still evolving), and backwards-compat shim for older Claude Code versions without MCP. (5) Risk: MCP plugin support is not yet stable in Claude Code — hooks API is the supported integration point. (6) Recommendation: defer MCP capture to a future milestone when Claude Code plugin MCP support stabilizes. Keep tag+file dual capture as current strategy. The nonce anti-spoofing (H-SEC-004) adequately mitigates tag injection.
PreCompact hook created — preserves flow summary, variables, gate status, and capture context across compaction
SessionStart hook now detects awaiting_capture state and re-emits capture prompts after compaction
debug.ts created with PROMPT_LANGUAGE_DEBUG env var support, debug logging added to session-start and user-prompt-submit hooks

Research complete: MCP flow_capture_response tool is viable but requires Claude Code MCP server integration. Current XML tag mechanism works. Would eliminate retry loops but adds deployment complexity. Deferred as lower priority than reliability fixes.
All 17 MAJOR sub-beads fixed and closed. 1 CRITICAL + 16 MAJOR defects resolved.
Fixed: renderNodeToDsl now escapes newlines in prompt/run text via .replace(/\n/g, ' ') to prevent DSL injection when spawning child processes.

Fixed: AutoAdvanceResult is now a tagged union with kind: 'prompt' | 'advance' | 'pause' discriminant. All return sites updated with correct kind. Backward-compatible: capturedPrompt field preserved for existing callers.
Fixed: Foreach re-entry now uses cached progress.maxIterations from NodeProgress instead of re-deriving via splitIterable, preventing mutation drift when loop body modifies the list variable.

Fixed: Changed timeoutSeconds \!= null to timeoutSeconds \!== undefined so that zero timeout (meaning 'no timeout') is not treated as a truthy value. Zero still correctly falls through to the timeoutSeconds > 0 guard.

Fixed: Removed node:crypto import from session-state.ts (domain layer). generateCaptureNonce now uses pure Math.random() to generate hex nonce, preserving domain zero-dependency constraint. createSessionState accepts optional captureNonce param.
Fixed: Capture nonce increased from randomBytes(4) = 32 bits (8 hex chars) to 128 bits (32 hex chars). Now uses 4 segments of Math.random() * 0x100000000 for sufficient entropy without crypto import.

{"id":"prompt-language-xw3p","title":"Fix operator precedence: arithmetic (* before +) and logic (and before or)","description":"Why: The DSL currently evaluates all arithmetic and logic left-to-right with no precedence. This silently produces wrong results: `let x = 2 + 3 * 4` yields 20 instead of 14. `if a or b and c` evaluates as (a or b) and c instead of the standard a or (b and c). Every developer carries precedence intuition from every other language they know — violating it creates bugs that are invisible in testing.

What: Implement standard mathematical precedence (* / % before + -) in evaluateArithmetic() and logical precedence (and binds tighter than or) in evaluateCondition(). Both are currently flat left-to-right.

Acceptance criteria:
- let x = 2 + 3 * 4 yields 14
- if a or b and c evaluates as a or (b and c)
- Lint warns on expressions that would evaluate differently under old vs new rules
- All existing tests still pass (add migration warning for changed semantics)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-26T01:53:18Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-03-26T01:53:18Z"}

{"id":"prompt-language-fh58","title":"Add parenthesized expressions in conditions: `if (a or b) and c`","description":"Why: Without parentheses, users cannot override evaluation order. Even after fixing precedence, parentheses are essential for any non-trivial boolean expression. This is a pure additive syntax change — no existing condition can contain literal ( or ) characters.

What: Modify evaluateCondition() to detect balanced parens, recurse on the inner expression, and continue with the result. ~30-50 LOC.

Acceptance criteria:
- if (a or b) and c works correctly
- Nested parens work: if ((a and b) or c) and d
- Unbalanced parens produce a lint warning, not a silent failure","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-26T01:53:27Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-03-26T01:53:27Z"}

{"id":"prompt-language-jdd3","title":"Hook error recovery: classify transient vs permanent errors, retry transient once","description":"Why: Current catch handlers in all hook entry points silently swallow ALL errors (filesystem errors, JSON parse errors, command runner crashes) and exit 0. The hook appears to succeed but did nothing. The user sees no error, the flow doesn't advance, and the next interaction hits the same error.

What: Classify errors as transient (EBUSY, EAGAIN, ECONNRESET) vs permanent (ENOENT, SyntaxError). Retry transient errors once with 100ms delay. Log permanent errors to audit trail with full stack trace. Never silently swallow — always log before exiting.

Acceptance criteria:
- Transient filesystem errors retry once automatically
- Permanent errors are logged to audit.jsonl with stack trace
- No error is silently swallowed
- Shared error-classification utility used across all 5 hook entry points","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-26T01:56:11Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-03-26T01:56:11Z"}

{"id":"prompt-language-ifof","title":"Flow-spec hash: detect stale state when flow definition changes mid-execution","description":"Why: If a user edits their prompt (adds a step, changes a gate) while a flow is active, the persisted currentNodePath indexes into a stale node tree. The runtime silently resolves to the wrong node or returns null, causing flows to skip steps or complete incorrectly. This is a data-corruption-class bug with no current detection.

What: Hash the parsed FlowSpec (nodes + gates) and store as flowSpecHash in SessionState. On each user-prompt-submit hook, re-parse and compare hashes. If they differ and a flow is active, warn and offer reset.

Acceptance criteria:
- Hash computed at parse time and stored in session state
- Mismatch detected on load, warning surfaced to user
- User can choose to reset or continue (at their own risk)
- ~30 LOC change","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-26T01:55:47Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-03-26T01:55:47Z"}

{"id":"prompt-language-jx2u","title":"Spawn child cleanup: SIGTERM orphaned children on parent flow failure/cancellation","description":"Why: When a parent flow fails or is cancelled, spawned child claude -p processes are orphaned. They continue consuming API tokens, CPU, and disk I/O indefinitely. With child.unref() and detached: true, there is zero cleanup path. In a flow with 5 spawns, a single parent failure leaves 5 zombie processes.

What: When parent flow transitions to failed or cancelled, iterate spawnedChildren and send SIGTERM to any still-running PIDs. Add cleanup in inject-context.ts cancellation paths and in the stop hook.

Acceptance criteria:
- Parent flow failure sends SIGTERM to all running spawned children
- PIDs that are no longer running are handled gracefully (process.kill(pid, 0) to check)
- Skip on Windows where SIGTERM behavior differs (document this)
- Test: spawn 2 children, fail parent, verify children exit","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-26T01:56:01Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-03-26T01:56:01Z"}
